body.title: [triage] Consequence check: minion worker + tiered escalation (2026-06-14)
body.description:
**â›” FAIL â€” KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** â€” it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK â€” IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix â€” never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 â€” FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triageâ†’sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract â€” *'I intend that X, because Y'* â€” where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' â€” it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact â€” the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

**What changed:** Minion worker device executes sprint tickets via cheap inference (DeepSeek V4 Flash) with DONE/ESCALATE tiered escalation baked into the system prompt.

**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) cheap model produces plausible-but-wrong diffs that pass tests but break behavior; (2) ESCALATE signals loop (minion escalates to worker which escalates back); (3) ticket close rate drops vs Claude baseline; (4) budget ledger underestimates cost if DeepSeek charges differ from registry pricing; (5) Granny's cap=1 means one stuck ticket blocks the whole queue

**Signals to watch:** budget ledger openrouter/deepseek spend per closed ticket; escalation tier distribution (what % reach analyst/designer); ticket close rate vs prior 10-ticket baseline; any ESCALATE: workerâ†’worker loops in daemon log

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-14 or after 10 tickets close through the worker.

**Affected files:** None â€” observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification â€” observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention â€” cannot be proven; design incomplete
